search_term: dark factory pattern infrastructure IaC autonomous
summary: infra/IaC: Pulumi proposes a 4-phase rollout of the Dark Factory pattern for autonomous IaC, citing StrongDM (32k LOC) and Stripe Minions (1000+ PRs/wk) as anchor cases and stressing tighter blast-radius gates than app code.

search_term: Stripe Minions autonomous coding agent payments
summary: fintech/payments + internal tooling: Stripe's Minions ship 1000+ PRs/week of one-shot, end-to-end agent-written code in their Ruby payments monorepo; sandboxed dev VMs, cap retries at 2 to avoid burning compute, all PRs still receive human review before merge.

search_term: Stripe Minions architecture deterministic agentic nodes
summary: fintech/payments: Part 2 of Stripe's Minions describes blueprint architecture mixing deterministic and agentic nodes for high-volume PR generation; reinforces internal-tooling dominance.

search_term: dark factory legal regulated accountability StrongDM
summary: regulated/legal commentary on security domain: Stanford CodeX argues StrongDM's choice to build security software with no human review creates accountability, disclosure, and warranty gaps; existing FTC, product-liability, and insurance frameworks have no category for unreviewed agent code.

search_term: vibe coding healthcare HIPAA PHI failure
summary: healthcare: Specode catalogs concrete failure modes of vibe-/agent-coded healthcare apps under HIPAA — plaintext PHI storage, missing audit logs, hallucinated encryption (encryptPHI() that's actually base64), no BAA coverage, JWTs without expiry.

search_term: vibe coding healthcare compliance time bomb
summary: healthcare: Mexico Business News frames vibe/dark-factory coding in healthcare as a 'compliance time bomb', citing low enterprise adoption (28% healthcare, 34% financial services) and patient-trust risks.

search_term: agentic pipelines embedded software engineering
summary: embedded/firmware: Sun & Staron (Chalmers, Jan 2026) qualitative study of 10 senior engineers across 4 embedded firms finds Dark-Factory-style autonomy blocked by non-determinism, certification gaps, and 10+ year traceability requirements ('we can check in the compiler from 10 years ago, but not the LLM').

search_term: agentic data engineering Databricks Genie autonomous
summary: ML/data engineering: Databricks Genie Code (April 2026) targets data-engineering pipelines but explicitly keeps human-in-the-loop on every change; full autonomy ('AI-optimized workloads' running unattended) is positioned as future work, not current capability.

search_term: dark factory pattern autonomous coding
summary: internal tooling/microservices: HackerNoon walkthrough of an 8-engineer platform team running a Dark Factory over ~12 microservices (Java/TypeScript/Spring Boot); claims 3-10x velocity, leans on holdout-scenario validation borrowed from StrongDM.

search_term: dark factory AI coding best fit domain
summary: domain decision rule: MindStudio enumerates Dark-Factory best-fit domains (migrations, refactors, dependency updates, boilerplate, test writing) vs explicit excludes (auth, payments, user data, core infra, novel business logic).

search_term: dark factory architecture pattern level 4
summary: architecture/IaC framing: Infralovers (Feb 2026) describes Dark Factory as Level-4/5 spec-driven architecture; concedes adoption is concentrated at Anthropic and StrongDM and that AI-accelerated brownfield work shows 48% speed gain but no improvement in codebase understanding.

search_term: vibe coding enterprise risks pitfalls
summary: enterprise/CRUD: Retool argues vibe/dark-factory coding is fine for prototypes and simple CRUD but breaks in production systems handling real data, multi-team permissions, or HIPAA/SOX/GDPR compliance.

search_term: Cognition Devin annual review production case
summary: fintech/enterprise migration: Cognition's 2025 Devin review documents Visma (fintech, doubled productivity), Nubank (6M LOC migration), Goldman Sachs, Santander, EightSleep (3x data features), Litera (40% test-coverage lift, 93% faster regressions), and Oracle Java migration (14x faster); failure modes are ambiguous specs and mid-task scope changes, not domain-specific.

search_term: Rakuten Claude Code autonomous 12.5M lines
summary: ML/infra (vLLM): Rakuten engineer ran Claude Code autonomously for 7 hours over a 12.5M-line vLLM codebase, hitting 99.9% numerical accuracy on an activation-vector extraction port; cited as the touchstone case for autonomous coding on large open-source ML infrastructure.

search_term: Anthropic 2026 agentic coding trends report
summary: cross-domain enterprise: Anthropic's 2026 trends report documents agent adoption beyond engineering (sales, legal, ops, marketing, security analysis); enterprise wins include Rakuten 99.9%/12.5M, TELUS 30% faster + 500k hours saved, Zapier 89-97% adoption with 800+ agents, CRED 2x execution.

search_term: Stripe Minions 1300 PRs internal tooling
summary: fintech/internal tooling: ByteByteGo's writeup on Stripe Minions confirms 1300 PRs/week with zero human-written code in payments monorepo; emphasizes isolated dev VMs and tool-budget pruning (~15 of 400+ tools).

search_term: dark factory engineering team practitioner reflection
summary: practitioner reflection across domains: Aditya treats the Dark Factory as an organizational pattern, arguing the bottleneck shifts to spec quality across all domains and listing high-risk excludes (user data, production infra, novel business logic).

search_term: StrongDM software factory security software
summary: security software (the original Dark Factory in production): StrongDM's own blog describes building privileged-access security software with agents that write and review all code; the most counter-intuitive domain for unreviewed code, made tractable by external scenario sets and Digital Twin Universe.

search_term: vibe coding security compliance fintech regulated
summary: fintech/regulated: Attract Group surveys security and compliance failure modes of vibe/dark-factory coding for fintech and regulated workloads; emphasizes audit-trail and access-control gaps that scale to compliance violations.

search_term: AutoMat coding agents reproduce computational materials science
summary: scientific computing/computational materials science: AutoMat benchmark of 85 SME-curated claims finds best agent succeeds only 54.1% end-to-end, failing on incomplete procedures, methodological deviations, and execution fragility — direct evidence Dark-Factory autonomy breaks in scientific reproducibility.
